description: Risk scoring algorithm review meeting with mixed bugs and features. Tests type classification, duplicate detection (NaN bug overlaps ERIS-012), and architecture constraint detection (SQLite single-writer limitation).

candidate_stories.title: Fix NaN display for empty documents and image-only PDFs
candidate_stories.description: When an empty document or image-only PDF is submitted, the risk score displays as NaN. The system should short-circuit before calling the LLM, return a score of 0 with category Minimal, and display a clear message.
acceptance_criteria: Empty text input returns score 0, category Minimal, with 'no text content' message
Image-only PDFs without OCR return the same graceful response
LLM API is not called when document text is empty
NaN is never displayed to the user in any scenario
priority_signals: Mike: 'That's a bug we need to fix'
Jordan confirmed it is already logged as ERIS-012
candidate_stories.source_citation: Jordan: 'If you submit an empty document — literally an empty text field — the risk score comes back as NaN. The UI just shows "NaN%" which is obviously not great.'

candidate_stories.title: Define explicit threshold boundary rules (lower-inclusive, upper-exclusive)
candidate_stories.description: Formalize the risk score threshold boundary behavior: lower bound inclusive, upper bound exclusive for each range. Document this in the code and configuration schema.
acceptance_criteria: Threshold boundaries are lower-inclusive, upper-exclusive (e.g., 40 is Low, 41 is Medium)
Boundary behavior is documented in the configuration schema
Code comments clarify the comparison operators used
Boundary edge cases are covered by automated tests
priority_signals: Mike made explicit decision in meeting
candidate_stories.source_citation: Mike: 'Let's make it explicit: the lower bound is inclusive, the upper bound is exclusive for each range. So Minimal is 0 to 20 inclusive, Low is 21 to 40 inclusive, and so on. Document that in the code and in the configuration schema.'

candidate_stories.title: Add server-side validation to clamp risk scores to 0-100
candidate_stories.description: Add server-side validation to ensure risk scores returned by the LLM are always within the 0-100 range. If the LLM returns a score outside this range, clamp it. If it returns a non-numeric score, flag it as an error.
acceptance_criteria: Scores below 0 are clamped to 0
Scores above 100 are clamped to 100
Non-numeric scores are flagged as errors with a user-visible message
Clamping events are logged for monitoring
priority_signals: Part of Decision 4 in meeting summary
candidate_stories.source_citation: Alex: 'We should also add validation on the server side. If the LLM returns a score outside 0-100, we clamp it. If it returns a non-numeric score, we flag it as an error rather than displaying NaN.'

candidate_stories.title: Add metadata fields to risk-category-rules.json for audit
candidate_stories.description: Add last-modified-by and last-modified-at fields to the risk-category-rules.json file to track who changed thresholds and when. This is a lightweight alternative to migrating thresholds to the database.
acceptance_criteria: risk-category-rules.json includes last-modified-by and last-modified-at fields
Fields are updated automatically on every threshold save
Existing threshold data is preserved during the format migration
priority_signals: Decision 3: thresholds stay in JSON with added metadata
candidate_stories.source_citation: Mike: 'Let's keep it in JSON for now and add that metadata. We can revisit the database migration if we get requirements for a full audit trail on configuration changes. Decision: thresholds stay in risk-category-rules.json with added last-modified-by and last-modified-at fields.'

candidate_stories.title: Add threshold validation to prevent overlapping or gapped ranges
candidate_stories.description: Validate threshold configurations on save to ensure no overlapping ranges, no gaps, and full coverage of the 0-100 spectrum.
acceptance_criteria: Saving overlapping threshold ranges returns a validation error
Saving thresholds with gaps returns a validation error
Thresholds must cover the full 0-100 spectrum
Validation runs on the settings save endpoint before persisting
priority_signals: Alex committed to adding this to acceptance criteria for settings endpoint
candidate_stories.source_citation: Alex: 'We need validation on the threshold save endpoint. No overlapping ranges, no gaps, must cover the full 0-100 spectrum. I'll add that to the acceptance criteria for the settings endpoint.'

candidate_stories.title: Document cross-provider score variation as known limitation
candidate_stories.description: Document that different LLM providers may return different risk scores for the same document and that this is expected behavior, not a bug. Organizations switching providers should recalibrate thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: Known limitation documented in system documentation
Documentation advises threshold recalibration when switching providers
No score normalization is implemented across providers
candidate_stories.source_citation: Mike: 'We're not going to normalize scores across providers. If an organization switches providers, they should recalibrate their thresholds.' Alex: 'OK. I'll document that as a known limitation.'

checks.story_title: Fix NaN display for empty documents and image-only PDFs
checks.details: This bug is already tracked as ERIS-012 ('Risk score shows NaN for empty documents') in the backlog with status 'in_progress'. The meeting discussion describes the same issue and its root cause (parseInt on null LLM response).
checks.proposed_resolution: Merge this meeting's additional context (short-circuit before LLM call, score 0 with Minimal category, cover image-only PDFs) into ERIS-012's acceptance criteria rather than creating a duplicate item.

checks.story_title: Add metadata fields to risk-category-rules.json for audit
checks.details: Meeting discussed migrating thresholds from JSON to SQLite (ERIS-013) but decided against it due to SQLite single-writer constraint. The architecture document confirms: 'SQLite supports only one concurrent write transaction' and 'Concurrent writes to the same JSON file can cause data loss (last-write-wins with no locking)'. The decision to keep JSON avoids the SQLite write contention but does not solve the JSON concurrent write risk.
checks.proposed_resolution: Keep thresholds in JSON as decided, but flag the concurrent JSON write risk from the architecture constraints. Consider adding file locking for the JSON write operation.

checks.story_title: Add metadata fields to risk-category-rules.json for audit
checks.details: This story's decision to keep thresholds in JSON effectively deprioritizes ERIS-013 ('Refactor risk category rules configuration to use database'), which proposes the opposite approach. ERIS-013 should be updated to reflect that database migration is deferred pending audit trail requirements.
checks.proposed_resolution: Update ERIS-013 status and description to note that the team decided to defer database migration in the risk scoring review meeting. Keep it in backlog as a future option.
